assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items focus on U.S. domestic politics (DHS shutdown, TSA pay) and Iran/Hormuz tensions; there is no report of a Trump–Xi in-person bilateral meeting in mainland China publicly confirmed by the White House or China’s MFA.
No USPS public notice or PRC filing mentioned today indicating implementation of an ≥8% fuel surcharge on any domestic product; nothing in the provided bullets resolves this.
No CPI release result (headline CPI-U SA m/m) is referenced in today’s bullets, so there is no evidence to mark the threshold event as having occurred.
News discusses oil and Strait of Hormuz dynamics and notes oil prices falling, but provides no confirmation that ICE Brent front-month futures settled ≥$115 on any trading day.
No Freddie Mac PMMS weekly print is cited; nothing confirms a 30-year fixed average ≥7.50% in any weekly release.
No Federal Reserve H.4.1 balance-sheet data is mentioned today; there is no evidence that the deferred asset line exceeded $250B.
No announcement of a new USTR Section 301 investigation naming China and covering consumer-goods categories appears in today’s bullets.

Meta is reported to have suffered court defeats, but there is no indication of an SEC-filed litigation settlement accrual/charge ≥$250M tied specifically to social-media addiction/harm-to-minors lawsuits.
